policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Retrofit Buildings' to reduce energy use and carbon emissions across residential, commercial, industrial, and public buildings.
verbatim_text: Chicago's residential, commercial, and industrial buildings account for nearly 69% of the City's carbon footprint. Reducing building energy use through efficiency will reduce the city's GHG emissions, save residents and businesses money, and improve the city's resiliency to climate shocks and stressors. Retrofits for most building types can include weatherization, smart thermostats, lighting and appliance replacements, and heating, ventilation, and air conditioning equipment upgrades.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: RETROFIT BUILDINGS
extraction_rationale: This is an umbrella strategy heading with multiple listed A–E actions beneath it; thus it qualifies as a 'parent' policy per hierarchy rules.

policy_statement: Retrofit 20% of residential buildings with 4 or fewer units by 2030 and 50% by 2040, prioritizing low- or moderate-income households.
verbatim_text: Retrofit residential buildings with 4 or fewer units: 20% by 2030 and 50% by 2040, prioritizing low- or moderate-income households. Smaller residential buildings, including the city's nearly 400,000 single-family homes and small multifamily properties, generated 14% of the city's total carbon emissions in 2017. To reach the City's 2030 goal, 78,500 smaller residential buildings must be retrofitted, and to reach the City's 2050 goal, 196,000 smaller residential buildings must be retrofitted.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: RETROFIT BUILDINGS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with quantified targets and deadlines (20% by 2030; 50% by 2040), prioritizing LMI households—meets extractability via quantifiable targets.

policy_statement: Retrofit 20% of total 5+ unit residential buildings by 2030.
verbatim_text: Retrofit 20% of total 5+ unit residential buildings by 2030. This action will bring improved efficiency and living conditions to nearly 140,000 units in large multifamily buildings.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: RETROFIT BUILDINGS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (20% by 2030).

policy_statement: Retrofit 20% of total industrial buildings by 2030.
verbatim_text: Retrofit 20% of total industrial buildings by 2030. Industrial buildings use large amounts of energy for manufacturing and other processes, comprising 16.6% of the City's 2017 carbon footprint.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: RETROFIT BUILDINGS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (20% by 2030).

policy_statement: Retrofit 90% of total City-owned and sister agency-owned buildings by 2035.
verbatim_text: Retrofit 90% of total city-owned and sister agency-owned buildings by 2035. City and sister agency buildings include public schools, community centers, libraries, fire and police stations, offices, and other buildings that serve the public.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: RETROFIT BUILDINGS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (90% by 2035).

policy_statement: Retrofit 20% of total commercial buildings by 2035.
verbatim_text: Retrofit 20% of total commercial buildings by 2035. Commercial buildings include offices, restaurants, and stores.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: RETROFIT BUILDINGS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (20% by 2035).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Connect Communities to Renewable Energy' to expand community-owned and community-based renewable energy access.
verbatim_text: Expanding innovative community ownership models for renewable energy projects on City property and utilizing state funding will help Chicago achieve 100% clean renewable energy community-wide by 2035. Connection to renewable energy can increase community resiliency and decrease household energy costs. The City will prioritize models that expand access to the most energy-burdened households.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–C actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Install 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects within city limits by 2025, prioritizing high energy-burden households.
verbatim_text: Install 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects by 2025. By 2025, 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects will be installed within city limits, prioritizing households with the highest energy burden.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (5 MW by 2025).

policy_statement: Increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts by 2025.
verbatim_text: Increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts by 2025. Expanding access to renewable energy for all Chicagoans starts by building energy infrastructure within the city limits.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (20 MW by 2025).

policy_statement: Increase community renewables subscriptions so that 25% are subscribed by low-income and/or environmental justice low-income residents by 2030.
verbatim_text: Increase community renewables subscriptions to achieve 25% subscribed by low-income and/or environmental justice low-income residents by 2030. Providing access to community renewables for low-income or environmental justice households reduces energy cost burdens.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (25% subscribed by 2030).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Reduce Waste and Landfilling' to advance circular materials management and diversion from landfills.
verbatim_text: Acknowledging that reducing overall waste provides the greatest benefits, it is also important to improve the convenience and effectiveness of waste management infrastructure, so that it is easier for individuals and businesses to divert waste from landfills. Reusing durable materials for as long as possible, improving recycling rates and addressing contamination issues, and composting organic waste are key starting points.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–F actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Introduce community-wide organic waste collection and decomposition by 2025.
verbatim_text: Introduce community-wide organic waste collection and decomposition by 2025. In households and restaurants, food waste is typically the largest portion of the waste stream. When sent to landfills, food waste causes detrimental methane emissions.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a specific intervention and deadline (by 2025).

policy_statement: Implement equitable waste source prevention strategies by 2030.
verbatim_text: Implement equitable waste source prevention strategies by 2030. Eliminating waste, particularly by avoiding single-use products, brings many benefits.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline and clear intervention (source prevention strategies by 2030).

policy_statement: Divert 90% of commercial, industrial, and institutional waste by 2030.
verbatim_text: Divert 90% of commercial, industrial, and institutional waste by 2030. To achieve a 90% diversion goal by 2030, the City will help commercial and industrial businesses implement new source reduction and prevention techniques and improve reuse and recycling.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable diversion target and deadline (90% by 2030).

policy_statement: Divert 75% of construction and demolition waste by 2030.
verbatim_text: Divert 75% of construction and demolition waste by 2030. By 2030, the city will divert 75% of construction and demolition waste from landfills and incineration.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable diversion target and deadline (75% by 2030).

policy_statement: Enable building design for disassembly and reuse by 2035.
verbatim_text: Enable building design for disassembly and reuse by 2035. Buildings can be designed as high performing and durable modular material assemblies that can be easily deconstructed by skilled professionals.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action specifying a code/design outcome with a deadline (by 2035).

policy_statement: Divert 90% of residential waste by 2040.
verbatim_text: Divert 90% of residential waste by 2040. This patchwork of practices creates challenges and can be improved by standardizing recycling practices and expanding composting options.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable diversion target and deadline (90% by 2040).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips' by expanding safe, connected, low-carbon mobility networks.
verbatim_text: Every neighborhood should have streets with the necessary infrastructure to ensure safe and comfortable travel for everyone. The network of protected lanes, neighborhood greenways, and off-street trails will continue to grow citywide, and traffic flows will continue to be evaluated to optimize bike routes for safety and convenience.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–C actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Expand high-quality and low-stress on-street bikeways and off-street trails, including 25 miles of protected lanes and 25 miles of neighborhood greenways by end of 2024, and double biking rates by 2030.
verbatim_text: Expand high-quality and low-stress on-street bikeways and off-street trails. By the end of 2024 the City aims to add 25 more miles of protected bike lanes, 25 more miles of neighborhood greenways, and meet the CDOT Strategic Plan goal of eliminating 15 miles of gaps in the citywide bikeways network. Through these and other investments that support community cycling, by 2030 the City aims to double current biking rates.
parent_policy_name: Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips
section_header: STRATEGY 1: MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with specific interventions and numeric build-out targets by 2024 and 2030.

policy_statement: Increase Divvy bikes and shared micromobility trips by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text: Increase Divvy bikes and shared micromobility trips 30% by 2030. Over 200 new Divvy bike stations will be delivered to the South, Southwest, West, and Northwest Sides.
parent_policy_name: Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips
section_header: STRATEGY 1: MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (30% by 2030).

policy_statement: Enable Chicagoans to walk, bike, take transit, or use shared micromobility for 45% of all trips by 2040.
verbatim_text: Enable Chicagoans to walk, bike, take transit, or use shared micromobility for 45% of all trips by 2040. From 2022 through 2024, the City intends to meet the CDOT Strategic Plan goals of designing and installing bus priority treatments along three corridors.
parent_policy_name: Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips
section_header: STRATEGY 1: MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (45% of all trips by 2040).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development' with policy and program changes to boost transit use.
verbatim_text: By increasing access to reliable transit, Chicagoans can reduce the number or distance of vehicle trips and related emissions. Walkable neighborhoods, accessible sidewalks, and transit-supportive improvements on key bus and rail corridors will increase transit ridership, reduce congestion and travel times, and improve air quality.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: INCREASE TRANSIT PERFORMANCE AND ENCOURAGE EQUITABLE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–E actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Update land use policies to encourage sustainable development, accessibility, and street safety by 2023.
verbatim_text: Update land use policies to encourage sustainable development, accessibility, and street safety by 2023. Chicago can expand and strengthen this land use policy to apply across the city more equitably and to promote a diversity of housing options and mixed-use developments near transit.
parent_policy_name: Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development
section_header: STRATEGY 2: INCREASE TRANSIT PERFORMANCE AND ENCOURAGE EQUITABLE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and defined policy intervention.

policy_statement: Expand use of commuter benefits by 2024.
verbatim_text: Expand use of commuter benefits by 2024. Providing incentives for workers to take transit can increase transit ridership.
parent_policy_name: Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development
section_header: STRATEGY 2: INCREASE TRANSIT PERFORMANCE AND ENCOURAGE EQUITABLE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2024) and specific intervention.

policy_statement: Require transportation demand management plans for new development by 2025.
verbatim_text: Require transportation demand management plans for new development by 2025. Transportation Demand Management (TDM) plans encourage the use of transit, bikes, and reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips while also reducing traffic congestion and associated emissions.
parent_policy_name: Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development
section_header: STRATEGY 2: INCREASE TRANSIT PERFORMANCE AND ENCOURAGE EQUITABLE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2025) and regulatory requirement.

policy_statement: Update citywide car and bike parking requirements by 2025.
verbatim_text: Update citywide car and bike parking requirements by 2025. The current zoning code parking minimums mandate that new developments build on-site car parking spaces based on 2004 zoning policy standards.
parent_policy_name: Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development
section_header: STRATEGY 2: INCREASE TRANSIT PERFORMANCE AND ENCOURAGE EQUITABLE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2025) and specific regulatory update.

policy_statement: Increase CTA ridership 20% by 2030 (relative to 2019 baseline).
verbatim_text: Increase CTA ridership 20% by 2030. The 20% increase that this action targets is based on pre-pandemic ridership (2019) though. CTA ridership must first rebound to pre-pandemic ridership levels before increasing.
parent_policy_name: Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development
section_header: STRATEGY 2: INCREASE TRANSIT PERFORMANCE AND ENCOURAGE EQUITABLE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (increase 20% by 2030).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Enable zero-emission transit and fleets' to electrify freight, ride-hail/taxi, delivery, municipal, and transit fleets.
verbatim_text: Zero-emission transit options are necessary to curbing emissions and improving local air quality. Shifting to electric taxis, ride hail fleet, and City vehicles will replace approximately 50,000 combustion-engine vehicles.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–E actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Enable electric freight loading docks at commercial and industrial buildings—address new buildings by 2025 and existing buildings by 2030.
verbatim_text: Enable electric freight loading docks at commercial and industrial buildings, addressing new buildings by 2025 and existing buildings by 2030. For new buildings, electric vehicle chargers must be considered during initial design and construction stages where implementation is easiest and most cost-effective.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with specific interventions and deadlines (new buildings by 2025; existing by 2030).

policy_statement: Support equitable electrification of ride-hail and taxi fleets by 2030.
verbatim_text: Support equitable electrification of ride-hail and taxi fleets by 2030. Ride-hail and taxi fleet electrification will reduce citywide emissions, reduce fuel costs for ride hail and taxi drivers, and improve air quality across the city.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable fleet-transition target and deadline (by 2030).

policy_statement: Enable 100% electrification of delivery fleets by 2035.
verbatim_text: Enable 100% electrification of delivery fleets by 2035. The last miles that packages travel from distribution centers to homes and businesses create significant GHG emissions and street-level air pollution.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable fleet-transition target and deadline (100% by 2035).

policy_statement: Electrify 100% of the City's fleet by 2035.
verbatim_text: Electrify 100% of the City's fleet by 2035. With a fleet of more than 9,400 vehicles... AIS is projected to convert 182 light-duty vehicles (25% of its fleet) by 2023.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable fleet-transition target and deadline (100% by 2035).

policy_statement: Achieve zero-emission transit fleets across Chicagoland by 2040.
verbatim_text: Achieve zero-emission transit fleets across Chicagoland by 2040. In 2019, the CTA committed to electrifying its entire bus fleet by 2040. Metra and PACE both aim to operate zero-emission fleets by 2040.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable fleet-transition target and deadline (by 2040).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy '100% Clean Renewable Energy' to achieve community-wide clean electricity and build local and regional renewable capacity.
verbatim_text: Installing and expanding in-city and in-state renewable energy will spur economic growth and create employment opportunities. Installing renewable projects on city property and utilizing state funding will help Chicago achieve its goal to achieve 100% clean, renewable energy community-wide by 2035.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–C actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Aggregate 5,000 megawatts of clean renewable energy within a 250-mile radius of Chicago by 2030.
verbatim_text: Aggregate 5,000 megawatts of clean renewable energy within a 250-mile radius of Chicago by 2030. This amount of clean energy can power more than 1.1 million Chicago homes each year.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable capacity target, geography, and deadline (5,000 MW within 250 miles by 2030).

policy_statement: Install 30 megawatts of clean renewable energy projects on City property by 2030.
verbatim_text: Install 30 megawatts of clean renewable energy projects on City property by 2030. Installing solar arrays on City-owned properties will ensure that previously underutilized space can reduce energy costs, increase local job opportunities, and improve citywide energy resiliency.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable capacity target and deadline (30 MW by 2030).

policy_statement: Achieve 100% clean renewable energy community-wide by 2035.
verbatim_text: Achieve 100% clean renewable energy communitywide by 2035. By 2035, the City will work with utilities to provide 100% clean renewable energy community-wide.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (100% community-wide by 2035).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification' to transition away from direct fossil fuel combustion in buildings and vehicles.
verbatim_text: Electrification refers to transitioning away from direct fossil-fuel combustion in equipment and vehicles by replacing fossil-fuel-powered equipment with alternatives that run on electricity. When paired with renewable electricity, equipment electrification significantly reduces GHG emissions.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with multiple A–F actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Enact policies that support electrified renovations and new construction by 2023.
verbatim_text: Enact policies that support electrified renovations and new construction by 2023. By 2023, the City will enable electrified renovations for all existing building types (residential, commercial, and industrial) and new construction.
parent_policy_name: Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and regulatory action.

policy_statement: Electrify 30% of total existing residential buildings by 2035.
verbatim_text: Electrify 30% of total existing residential buildings by 2035. Residential building electrification is one of the most important climate actions Chicagoans can take that also provides immediate health benefits for households.
parent_policy_name: Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (30% by 2035).

policy_statement: Electrify 20% of total existing industrial buildings by 2035.
verbatim_text: Electrify 20% of total existing industrial buildings by 2035. For other process with lower temperature requirements and moderate heating, ventilation, and air conditioning needs, using electric boilers, electric furnaces, heat pumps, and electric evaporation equipment are feasible.
parent_policy_name: Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (20% by 2035).

policy_statement: Electrify 10% of total existing commercial buildings by 2035.
verbatim_text: Electrify 10% of total existing commercial buildings by 2035. The ease of electrifying commercial buildings depends on many factors, including building size.
parent_policy_name: Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (10% by 2035).

policy_statement: Electrify 90% of total existing City-owned buildings by 2035.
verbatim_text: Electrify 90% of total existing City-owned buildings by 2035. City leadership in implementing building electrification will enable installers to invest in workforce training and technology partnerships, making it easier for private building owners to also transition to electric equipment.
parent_policy_name: Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (90% by 2035).

policy_statement: Enable 2,500 new public passenger electric vehicle charging stations by 2035, prioritizing low- and middle-income communities.
verbatim_text: Enable 2,500 new public passenger electric vehicle charging stations by 2035. In coordination with private and public electric vehicle charger operators and building owners, the City will enable the installation of 2,500 new commercial-grade, Level 2 public passenger electric vehicle charging stations by 2035, with priority given to low- and middle-income communities.
parent_policy_name: Enable Building and Personal Vehicle Electrification
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable target and deadline (2,500 stations by 2035).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Align building codes and standards with climate best practices' to advance green infrastructure and net-zero construction.
verbatim_text: To support Chicago's transition to a low-carbon future, the City must modernize its planning, zoning, and building codes. City planning policies and regulations must be aligned with goals and priorities outlined in the 2022 CAP.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ALIGN BUILDING CODES AND STANDARDS WITH CLIMATE BEST PRACTICES
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with A–B actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Strengthen policies that support installation of green roofs and walls, tree planting, and other vegetative cover by 2023.
verbatim_text: Strengthen policies that support installation of green roofs and walls, tree planting, and other vegetative cover by 2023. By 2023, the City will enable green-roof and green-wall installations, tree planting, and other vegetative cover on both existing buildings and new construction.
parent_policy_name: Align building codes and standards with climate best practices
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ALIGN BUILDING CODES AND STANDARDS WITH CLIMATE BEST PRACTICES — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and specific interventions (green roofs/walls, trees, vegetative cover).

policy_statement: Enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040, including targeting embodied carbon.
verbatim_text: Enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040. Net-zero construction standards can dramatically reduce built environment GHG emissions. In addition to targeting energy-related emissions once buildings are in use, net-zero-carbon construction incentives will also target embodied carbon.
parent_policy_name: Align building codes and standards with climate best practices
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ALIGN BUILDING CODES AND STANDARDS WITH CLIMATE BEST PRACTICES — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a clear target and deadline (enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Decommission Fossil Power' to transition away from fossil-fueled peaker plants through a planned, equitable approach.
verbatim_text: Illinois continues to rely on fossil-fueled power plants for backup power generation when energy demand is extremely high. The City must also look to where our energy is being produced and how it is being produced.
section_header: STRATEGY 4: DECOMMISSION FOSSIL POWER
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with one lettered action; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Develop a fossil-fuel plants (peaker plants) transition strategy by 2024 to guide an equitable, safe, and inclusive process.
verbatim_text: Develop a fossil-fuel plants transition strategy by 2024. By 2024, the City will develop a peaker plant (power plant used only when there is a high demand for electricity) transition strategy with guidance for an equitable, safe, and inclusive process.
section_header: STRATEGY 4: DECOMMISSION FOSSIL POWER — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2024) and explicit deliverable (transition strategy).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Enable Interconnection and Storage' to expand energy storage and demand response for a reliable clean grid.
verbatim_text: To ensure that cleaning our electric grid enhances power reliability and resiliency, Chicago will increase energy storage and distribution, and will promote better demand response during periods of peak electricity usage.
section_header: STRATEGY 5: ENABLE INTERCONNECTION AND STORAGE
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with A–B actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Ensure 150 megawatts of energy storage by 2025.
verbatim_text: Ensure 150 megawatts of energy storage by 2025. Building storage capacity adds grid flexibility and reliability and has air quality benefits.
section_header: STRATEGY 5: ENABLE INTERCONNECTION AND STORAGE — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a quantifiable capacity target and deadline (150 MW by 2025).

policy_statement: Invest in 1,000 megawatts of demand response by 2030 and 3,000 megawatts by 2040.
verbatim_text: Invest in 1,000 megawatts of demand response by 2024, and 3,000 megawatts by 2035. By achieving 1,000 megawatts of new energy demand reduction capacity by 2030, and increasing to 3,000 megawatts by 2040, Chicagoans will be better protected during extreme weather events.
section_header: STRATEGY 5: ENABLE INTERCONNECTION AND STORAGE — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with quantifiable targets and deadlines (1,000 MW and 3,000 MW by specified years).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Collect Relevant Data' to track energy burden and environmental quality at community levels.
verbatim_text: Energy burden, water quality, soil quality, and air quality data can be used to measure the equity of climate action across Chicago neighborhoods. Data will be collected by community area, where possible, to show decision-makers where and how their decisions most affect residents.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: COLLECT RELEVANT DATA
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with A–C actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Report energy burden by community area by 2023.
verbatim_text: Report energy burden by community area by 2023. The City will analyze energy reports and other data sources to identify where building retrofits or energy subsidies are most needed.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: COLLECT RELEVANT DATA — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and explicit reporting output.

policy_statement: Develop a water and soil quality measurement and mitigation strategy by 2023.
verbatim_text: Develop a water and soil quality measurement and mitigation strategy by 2023. In partnership with City-operated facilities, sister agencies, and community-based organizations, proactive measurement, public reporting, and application of water and soil quality data can inform where infrastructure improvements are needed.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: COLLECT RELEVANT DATA — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and explicit strategy deliverable.

policy_statement: Establish a robust outdoor air quality monitoring network by 2025.
verbatim_text: Establish a robust outdoor air quality monitoring network by 2025. Measuring and reporting air quality at various sites such as CTA bus and 'L' stations, schools, or city facilities will demonstrate the impact of the City's climate investments.
section_header: STRATEGY 1: COLLECT RELEVANT DATA — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2025) and explicit intervention (monitoring network).

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Enable Data-Driven Decision-Making' by integrating health, resilience, climate, and equity criteria into planning and budgeting.
verbatim_text: Achieving equity across neighborhoods will require that City policymakers and implementers have relevant and accurate data when making decisions. By integrating decision-making with equity criteria, City decisions can be made to objectively prioritize health outcomes.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE DATA-DRIVEN DECISION-MAKING
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with A–E actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Publish clean energy just transition metrics by 2022.
verbatim_text: Publish clean energy just transition metrics by 2022. The transition from fossil fuels to a clean-energy economy will certainly bring significant health and environmental benefits.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE DATA-DRIVEN DECISION-MAKING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2022) and explicit deliverable.

policy_statement: Integrate community resilience and climate justice criteria into department-level strategic planning and annual budget setting by 2023.
verbatim_text: Integrate community resilience and climate justice criteria into department-level strategic planning and annual budget setting by 2023. The City will work with stakeholders to identify key health, resilience, climate, and equity criteria to assess.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE DATA-DRIVEN DECISION-MAKING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and explicit integration into departmental planning and budget.

policy_statement: Develop a Heat Vulnerability Index and integrate it into planning and public health processes beginning in 2023.
verbatim_text: Develop a Heat Vulnerability Index and integrate into planning and development, community safety, and public health planning processes beginning in 2023. Climate science indicates that Chicagoland will continue to experience more days of extreme heat and increased precipitation.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE DATA-DRIVEN DECISION-MAKING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a start date (beginning in 2023) and explicit deliverable (Heat Vulnerability Index).

policy_statement: Publish citywide and community-level quality-of-life metrics on equity and sustainability by 2023.
verbatim_text: Publish citywide and community-level quality-of-life metrics on equity and sustainability by 2023. The City will work with community groups and frontline leaders to identify metrics that meaningfully reflect community needs, aspirations, and well-being.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE DATA-DRIVEN DECISION-MAKING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and explicit reporting scope.

policy_statement: Assess and optimize community-level emergency management strategies related to climate preparedness by 2024.
verbatim_text: Assess and optimize community-level emergency management strategies related to climate preparedness by 2024. While a climate hazard may affect the greater city or region, its impact may be unequally felt due to communities' underlying physical, socioeconomic, and health conditions.
section_header: STRATEGY 2: ENABLE DATA-DRIVEN DECISION-MAKING — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2024) and explicit assessment and optimization objective.

policy_statement: Chicago will implement the Strategy 'Enable Community Resiliency' to resource community-led projects and integrate resiliency into hazard mitigation.
verbatim_text: By co-designing resiliency projects with community members, local civic leaders, and businesses, initiatives can stem from a shared understanding of historic events, community needs, and other critical insights unique to that community.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE COMMUNITY RESILIENCY
extraction_rationale: Strategy heading with A–B actions; qualifies as 'parent'.

policy_statement: Resource community-led climate infrastructure projects by 2022.
verbatim_text: Resource community-led climate infrastructure projects by 2022. Pairing citywide infrastructure investments with community-designed resiliency projects creates more effective and relevant projects to those living within a community.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE COMMUNITY RESILIENCY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2022) and explicit resource allocation action.

policy_statement: Integrate community resiliency strategies with the City's hazard mitigation planning by 2023.
verbatim_text: Integrate community resiliency strategies with the City's hazard mitigation planning by 2023. As part of the next planning cycle, the Office of Emergency Management and Communications (OEMC) will continue to explicitly develop resiliency goals and metrics.
section_header: STRATEGY 3: ENABLE COMMUNITY RESILIENCY — ACTIONS
extraction_rationale: Lettered action with a deadline (by 2023) and explicit integration into hazard mitigation planning.

policy_statement: Reduce Chicago’s GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 (from 2017 baseline), prioritizing equity and environmental justice.
verbatim_text: The 2022 CAP aims to chart an equitable path to reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040. Following the principles defined in this plan, this pathway prioritizes improving the lives of all Chicagoans by promoting environmental justice, generating household savings, improving community health, transitioning away from fossil-fuel dependency, and ensuring that all Chicago communities are prepared for the undeniable impacts of climate change, by 2040.
section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS
extraction_rationale: Cross-cutting citywide target with a quantifiable reduction and deadline; a core policy commitment.

policy_statement: Do not count carbon offset credits toward achieving the 62% GHG reduction goal; focus first on reducing emissions within Chicago.
verbatim_text: Chicago will not count offset credits toward its 62% GHG reduction goal achievement. Furthermore, the City will reduce its own emissions before taking action beyond its borders.
section_header: The Role of Offsets
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy constraint on use of offsets; clear binding rule framing target accounting.

policy_statement: Invest $46 million to plant 75,000 trees over 5 years in underserved community areas.
verbatim_text: Expand Canopy Coverage — $46 million — Plant 75,000 trees over 5 years in underserved community areas. Expected Co-Benefits: • Reduced pollution burden • Increased home value • Lower energy burden • Improved thermal comfort • Increased carbon sink • Stormwater absorption • Reduced urban heat island.
section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
extraction_rationale: Funded initiative with explicit dollar allocation, deliverable (75,000 trees) and timeline (5 years) — resource allocation and quantifiable target.
